name: Diana Krall
description: Canadian recording artist; jazz singer and pianist
abstract: Diana Jean Krall  (born November 16, 1964) is a Canadian jazz pianist and singer known for her contralto vocals. She has sold more than 15 million albums worldwide, including over six million in the U.S.

about_quotes.speaker: John Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: And then, and Diana Krall was the music director and Diana and I had a lot of fun because you know we did “The Christmas Song” for another record at the same date
about_quotes.doc_title: John Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 18, 2023 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Shelly Berg
about_quotes.evidence: I think that’s certainly speaks to the appeal of Diana Krall and some of — you know she sold over a million CDs.
about_quotes.doc_title: Shelly Berg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 15, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Broadbent
about_quotes.evidence: She studied with me one summer when she came down from Canada.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Broadbent interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 26, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Diana Krall as an example of a musician from a later generation ('Roy Hargrove or Joshua Redman and others, Diana Krall'), but the interviewee does not mention her personally or describe any interaction.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jane Monheit
about_quotes.evidence: I remember seeing in the press somewhere Diana Krall getting some flack about her age at that time... If you are a big star with just amazing success, you know, a Harry Connick Jr. or a Diana Krall
about_quotes.doc_title: Jane Monheit interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, August 20, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Sheldon
about_quotes.evidence: We just got through working the AT&T Golf Tournament up there with Clint Eastwood... Diana Krall and Glen Campbell and Michael Bolton, and Clint Eastwood and his wife and my band
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Sheldon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999
